name: Becky G
description:
'''Rebbeca Marie Gomez'''{{r|latimes|"21under21"}} (born March 2, 1997), known professionally as '''Becky G''', is an American singer and actress. Gomez first gained recognition in 2011 for her [[Cover version|cover]] versions of popular songs, which she uploaded to [[YouTube]]. One of her videos caught the attention of record producer [[Dr. Luke]], who signed her to [[Kemosabe Records]] in a joint venture with [[RCA Records]]. Her debut single "Becky from the Block" and her debut [[extended play|EP]], ''[[Play It Again (EP)|Play It Again]]'', were released in 2013, although both failed to chart. In 2014, Gomez achieved mainstream success with the release of "[[Shower (song)|Shower]]", which reached the top twenty on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]]. In 2016, she released her first full Spanish-language single "[[Sola (Becky G song)|Sola]]" as her debut into the [[Latin American music in the United States|Latin music market]]. 

Gomez has released four studio albums. The first of these, ''[[Mala Santa]]'' (2019), and reached the top five of the [[Hot Latin Songs]] with its singles "[[Mayores]]" and "[[Sin Pijama]]", while "[[Cuando Te Besé]]" topped on the [[Argentina Hot 100]]. She followed it with ''[[Esquemas]]'' (2022), which contained "[[Mamiii]]", her first number-one on the Hot Latin Songs. Her third and fourth efforts, the [[Regional Mexican]] albums, ''[[Esquinas (album)|Esquinas]]'' (2023) and ''[[Encuentros]]'' (2024), produced the [[Billboard regional Mexican charts#Regional Mexican Airplay|Regional Mexican Airplay]] number-one singles "[[Por el Contrario]]" and "[[Mercedes (Becky G and Oscar Maydon song)|Mercedes]]". Gomez made her feature film debut in the superhero film ''[[Power Rangers (film)|Power Rangers]]'' (2017). She provided her voice in the animated fantasy film ''[[Gnome Alone]]'' (2017), and the DC superhero film ''[[Blue Beetle (film)|Blue Beetle]]'' (2023).

Gomez has received [[List of awards and nominations received by Becky G|various accolades]], including three [[American Music Awards]], a [[Billboard Latin Music Awards|''Billboard'' Latin Music Award]], nine [[Latin American Music Awards]], five [[Lo Nuestro Awards]], three [[People's Choice Awards]], and seven [[Premios Juventud]]. She founded the cosmetic company Treslúce Beauty (2021-2024). She was named on [[Forbes 30 Under 30|''Forbes'' 30 Under 30]] (2023) and [[Time 100|''Time'' 100]] (2025), and has received various honors, including ''[[Billboard (magazine)|Billboard]]''<nowiki/>'s [[Billboard Women in Music#Impact Award|Impact Award]] (2023) and [[Billboard Latin Women in Music#Global Impact Award|Global Impact Award]] (2026). Outside of music and acting, Gomez advocates for [[Immigration|immigrant rights]], [[gender equality]], [[mental health]] and the empowerment of the [[Hispanic and Latino Americans|Latino community]].
